title: Writing: Delegating and Reporting
learningGoal: Transform active sentences into passive and causative forms accurately.
instructions: You must write three distinct types of sentences: Active, Causative, and Passive. Pay close attention to the verb endings 'waa' (causative) and 'gaya/jayega' (passive).
writingPrompt:
Translate the following sentences into Hindi:
1. Active: I am doing the work. (I = Main, Work = Kaam, Doing = Kar raha hoon)
2. Causative (Delegation): I am getting the work done. (Getting done = Karwa raha hoon)
3. Passive (Past): The work was done. (Work = Kaam, Was done = Kiya gaya)
4. Passive (Future): The work will be done tomorrow. (Tomorrow = Kal, Will be done = Kiya jayega)
promptGoal: Demonstrate the ability to shift focus between the actor, the delegator, and the action itself.
referenceHints: Sentence 1: Main kaam kar raha hoon.
Sentence 2: Main kaam karwa raha hoon.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: मैं काम कर रहा हूँ। मैं काम करवा रहा हूँ। काम किया गया। कल काम किया जाएगा।
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Main kaam kar raha hoon. Main kaam karwa raha hoon. Kaam kiya gaya. Kal kaam kiya jayega.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: I am doing the work. I am getting the work done. The work was done. Tomorrow the work will be done.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect mapping of the four states: Active, Causative, Past Passive, and Future Passive.

evaluationCriteria.description: Sentence 2 MUST use 'karwa' to indicate delegation.
Sentence 3 MUST use 'gaya' as the helper verb for the past passive.
Sentence 4 MUST use 'jayega' as the helper verb for the future passive.
None of the passive sentences should use forms of 'Hona' (e.g., 'Kaam kiya tha' or 'Kaam kiya hoga'). They must use 'Jaana'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: मैं काम कर रहा हूँ। मैं काम करवा रहा हूँ। काम किया गया। कल काम किया जाएगा।
acceptableAlternates: Main kaam kar raha hoon. Main kaam karwa raha hoon. Kaam kiya gaya. Kal kaam kiya jayega.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Strict check on 'waa', 'gaya', and 'jayega'.

criteria.name: Passive Error
criteria.description: If user writes 'Kaam kiya tha' instead of 'Kaam kiya gaya', deduct heavily. 'Kiya tha' is active voice ('Someone had done the work').

feedbackRules.onPass: Flawless! You successfully navigated between active, delegating, and passive voices.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review Sections 1 and 4. Remember that the passive voice in Hindi uses 'Jaana' (to go). The past tense of Jaana is 'Gaya'. The future is 'Jayega'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Pay close attention to sentence 2. 'Kar' means to do. 'KarWA' means to get someone else to do it.

hintText: For sentence 3, literally translate 'Work went done'. For sentence 4, translate 'Tomorrow work will go done'.
commonMistakes: Translating 'The work was done' as 'Kaam kiya tha'.
